L. 112–154, title II, § 204(c)126 Stat. 1177Aug. 6, 2012“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall not be construed to decrease the aggregate amount of assistance available to an individual under the sections described in subsection (b) [set out as an Effective Date of 2012 Amendment note above], as most recently increased by the Secretary pursuant to section 2102(e) of such title [].”38 U.S.C. 2102(e)

children.text: where the individual elects to construct a housing unit on land to be acquired by such individual, the Secretary shall pay not to exceed 50 percent of the total cost to the individual of (A) the housing unit and (B) the necessary land upon which it is to be situated;

where the individual elects to construct a housing unit on land acquired by such individual prior to application for assistance under this chapter, the Secretary shall pay not to exceed the smaller of the following sums: (A) 50 percent of the total cost to the individual of the housing unit and the land necessary for such housing unit, or (B) 50 percent of the cost to the individual of the housing unit plus the full amount of the unpaid balance, if any, of the cost to the individual of the land necessary for such housing unit;

where the individual elects to remodel a dwelling which is not adapted to the requirements of such individual’s disability, acquired by such individual prior to application for assistance under this chapter, the Secretary shall pay not to exceed (A) the cost to the individual of such remodeling; or (B) 50 percent of the cost to the individual of such remodeling; plus the smaller of the following sums: (i) 50 percent of the cost to the individual of such dwelling and the necessary land upon which it is situated, or (ii) the full amount of the unpaid balance, if any, of the cost to the individual of such dwelling and the necessary land upon which it is situated; and

where the individual has acquired a suitable housing unit, the Secretary shall pay not to exceed the smaller of the following sums: (A) 50 percent of the cost to the individual of such housing unit and the necessary land upon which it is situated, or (B) the full amount of the unpaid balance, if any, of the cost to the individual of such housing unit and the necessary land upon which it is situated.

the actual cost, or, in the case of an individual acquiring a residence already adapted with special features, the fair market value, of the adaptations determined by the Secretary under such section 2101(b) to be reasonably necessary, or

children.label: c
children.text: The amount of assistance afforded under subsection (a) for an individual authorized assistance by shall not be reduced by reason that title to the housing unit, which is vested in the individual, is also vested in any other person, if the individual resides in the housing unit.section 2101(a) of this title
The aggregate amount of assistance available to an individual under shall be limited to $98,492.section 2101(a) of this title
The aggregate amount of assistance available to an individual under shall be limited to $19,733.section 2101(b) of this title
Subject to subsection (f), no veteran may receive more than six grants of assistance under this chapter.

Effective on October 1 of each year (beginning in 2009), the Secretary shall increase the amounts described in subsection (b)(2) and paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (d) in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the residential home cost-of-construction index for the preceding calendar year, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the residential home cost-of-construction index for the year preceding the year described in subparagraph (A).

The Secretary shall establish a residential home cost-of-construction index for the purposes of this subsection. The index shall reflect a uniform, national average change in the cost of residential home construction, determined on a calendar year basis. The Secretary may use an index developed in the private sector that the Secretary determines is appropriate for purposes of this subsection.

Beginning , notwithstanding the aggregate amounts specified in subsection (d), a covered veteran may apply for and receive an additional amount of assistance under subsection (a) or (b) of in an amount that does not exceed half of the amount specified in subsection (d).October 1, 2030section 2101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is described in subsection (a)(2) or (b)(2) of ;section 2101 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: as of the date of the veteran’s application for assistance under paragraph (1), most recently received assistance under this chapter more than 10 years before such date; and

children.label: C
children.text: lives in a home that the Secretary determines does not have adaptations that are reasonably necessary because of the veteran’s disability.

children.label: A
children.text: the amounts to recover, if any; and

children.label: B
children.text: who is liable to the United States for such overpayment.

children.label: i
children.text: under this chapter; or

children.label: ii
children.text: under chapter 31 of this title who is pursuing a rehabilitation program under such chapter in acquiring adaptations to a residence.

children.label: B
children.text: An owner or seller of real estate used, or intended to be used, in connection with assistance under this chapter.

children.label: C
children.text: A builder, contractor, supplier, tradesperson, corporation, trust, partnership, or other person, who provided services or goods relating to assistance under this chapter.

children.label: D
children.text: An attorney, escrow agent, or financial institution, that receives, or holds in escrow, funds relating to assistance under this chapter.

children.label: E
children.text: A surviving spouse, heir, assignee, or successor in interest of or to, any person described in this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: Any overpayment referred to in this subsection may be recovered in the same manner as any other debt due the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: In recovering the overpayment, the Secretary may charge administrative costs, fees, and interest, as appropriate, in a manner similar to the authority under .section 5315 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The recovery of any overpayment referred to in this subsection may be waived by the Secretary.

children.label: B
children.text: Waiver of any such overpayment as to a person described in paragraph (2) shall in no way release any other person described in such paragraph from liability.

The Secretary shall waive recovery under this subsection of any overpayment to a person described in paragraph (2)(A), or a dependent or survivor of such person, that arises from administrative error described in paragraph (7)(A).
Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as precluding the imposition of any civil or criminal liability under this title or any other law.

children.label: A
children.text: administrative error that results in an individual receiving assistance to which that individual is not entitled;

children.label: i
children.text: perform or allow to be performed any act relating to assistance under this chapter; or

children.label: ii
children.text: compensate any party performing services or supplying goods relating to assistance under this chapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: any disbursement of funds relating to assistance under this chapter, that, in the sole discretion of the Secretary, constitutes a misuse of such assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: notice of the finding by the Secretary of such overpayment;

children.label: B
children.text: a reasonable opportunity for such person to remedy the circumstances that effectuated the overpayment; and

children.label: C
children.text: a reasonable opportunity for such person to present evidence to the Secretary that an overpayment was not made.

For the purposes of , a decision to collect an overpayment from a person other than a person described in paragraph (2)(A), or a dependent or survivor of such person, may not be treated as a decision that affects the provision of benefits.section 511 of this title
